Poseidon Networking — Firewall

Firewall rules in Poseidon Networking are organised into chains: INPUT, OUTPUT, and FORWARD. Each chain processes packets through an ordered list of match-action rules. Matches can filter by source IP, destination IP, port, protocol, and connection state. NAT rules are configured in a separate chain. Poseidon Networking firewall chains include INPUT, OUTPUT, FORWARD, and PREROUTING. Poseidon Networking match rules filter by source IP, destination IP, protocol, port, and connection state.
